brand_dna.category: Independent women's footwear and leather bags - beautiful, considered flats for creative women.
brand_dna.core_belief: That women deserve beautiful shoes made for real life, moving through the day with grace, comfort and a sense of humour. Their stance: 'I Only Wear Flats'.
proof_points: Female-owned and led, established 2013 by Kerryn Moscicki; family owned and self funded
Made in small quantities to avoid overproduction and improve each iteration
Made in Guangdong, China with a small handful of factories chosen for shared values
Free shipping and free returns, plus a 30 day returns window
brand_dna.positioning: Premium, considered and playful - design-led flats with function, priced $229-$469, not fast fashion.

voice_pillars.name: Joyful and Irreverent
voice_pillars.desc: Writes with warmth and humour, celebrating creativity over convention.
voice_pillars.quote: the aesthetic is positive and joyful, combining a sense of humour and irreverence with function

voice_pillars.name: Considered and Intentional
voice_pillars.desc: Frames products as deliberate, small-batch and made with care, not disposable.
voice_pillars.quote: make products in small quantities to avoid overproduction and test and improve each iteration

voice_pillars.name: Real-Life Honest
voice_pillars.desc: Speaks plainly to the true needs of customers and admits the tricky parts of buying shoes online.
voice_pillars.quote: We know how tricky online shoe shopping is, so don't despair

voice_pillars.name: Poetic Product Storytelling
voice_pillars.desc: Gives each style a narrative and a ritual, elevating it beyond a spec sheet.
voice_pillars.quote: reimagined for the ritual of self-expression

phrases: I love this bag. Comfortable to carry and fits my book too.
walking-on-clouds comfort for your cross-city commute
a soft, unstructured companion for the modern creative
honour the ritual of the analogue

icp: Creative, independent-minded women who value beautiful design and everyday comfort, and who buy flats they can wear through real life rather than trend-chasing heels.

personas.name: The Creative Regular
personas.description: An artistic professional who lives in flats and wants shoes that look considered and feel good all day - move her with comfort plus design credibility.

personas.name: The Intentional Buyer
personas.description: Values small-batch, limited-edition, self-funded independence over mass production - move her with craftsmanship, scarcity and the brand's ethos.

personas.name: The Analogue Romantic
personas.description: Wants objects with meaning and a story, drawn to the 'life without screens' idea - move her with ritual, narrative and slow-living framing.

products_ladder.title: Light Years Hiker | She Gave Me Flowers
Life Without Screens Leather Tote | Aubergine

offers: FREE standard shipping on all orders $249 and over (AU)
$12 standard / $15 express / $20 same-day local delivery (within 10km) options
Free returns with a 30 day returns window
Buy Now Pay Later via AfterPay or Zip Pay (4 payments)
Click & Collect from North Melbourne and Vermont; optional Shipping Protection
Newsletter for early product access, exclusive updates and events

allowed.claim: Female-owned and led, independent brand established in 2013
Made in small quantities to avoid overproduction
Life Without Screens Tote is Limited Edition - only 50 made, crafted from premium Aubergine leather
allowed.source: Life Without Screens Tote product page

allowed.claim: Free shipping and free returns, with a 30 day returns window
Light Years Hiker features removable insoles and a spacious upper

banned.why: FTC Green Guides / ACCC - unqualified sustainability claim; small-batch production is not the same as a verified environmental claim

banned.claim: Health/therapeutic claims such as 'improves posture', 'fixes foot pain' or 'orthopedic'
banned.why: Health-benefit claims are tightly limited and unsupported by the site; keep comfort language experiential (e.g. 'walking-on-clouds')

banned.claim: 'Genuine leather' on styles not confirmed as leather (e.g. neoprene trainers)
banned.why: Material claims must match the actual product; only apply to items the site states are leather
No certification is stated; the site only says factories share the brand's values, which is not a certification
No lifetime warranty is offered; overstates the stated 30 day returns and product claims
Many products are leather; a blanket vegan claim would be false and misleading
